candidates.name: lagged-market-trend-regime-consistency

economic_rationale: A credible long/cash strategy should not require only an established rising market or only a flat/falling market to produce positive net returns. The regime uses only asset returns available before each evaluated session.
failure_reasons: BTC-USDT positive_trend 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.077627311150
BTC-USDT nonpositive_trend 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.158886683172
ETH-USDT positive_trend 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.133410893689
ETH-USDT nonpositive_trend 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.147805697151
hypothesis: For both BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT, net rolling OOS returns have a positive conditional annualized arithmetic mean when the prior 90-day compounded asset return is positive and when it is non-positive, with every 95% moving-block-bootstrap lower bound above zero.
limitations: BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT are development markets, not untouched holdouts.
The trend split is a descriptive mechanism diagnostic, not a trading rule.
Moving-block concatenation creates artificial joins; trend is recomputed after resampling.
The analysis does not model spread, impact, capacity, latency, or partial fills.

method.metric: conditional daily arithmetic mean multiplied by 365
method.regime_definition: positive_trend if compounded asset return over the previous 90 sessions is above zero; otherwise nonpositive_trend
method.resampling: asset-return and strategy-return paired non-circular moving blocks; lagged trend recomputed inside every sample
